mission.title: helm: Nested `null` values do not remove keys from sub-charts
mission.description: Nested `null` values do not remove keys from sub-charts. This issue affects 84+ users.

steps.title: Identify helm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your helm deployment:
```bash
helm version
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
helm version
```
#2648 and #5184 allow for deletion of keys by setting null values in a values.yaml file.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Nested `null` values do not remove keys from sub-charts
steps.description:
For the general pattern like this, using `false` works, although Helm complains about replacing a table with a boolean. 
```
      {{- if .Values.redis.securityContext }}
      securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.redis.securityContext | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}
```

Helm says it "cannot"
```yaml
kibana:
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 1000m
      memory: 2Gi
    limits:
      cpu: null
      memory: 2Gi
```

steps.title: Confirm Nested `null` values do not remove keys from… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
helm version
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

codeSnippets:
kibana:
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 1000m
      memory: 2Gi
    limits:
      cpu: null
      memory: 2Gi

apiVersion: v2
name: elastic
description: Elastic stack
type: application
version: 0.0.1
dependencies:
  - name: kibana
    version: 7.9.3
    repository: "@elastic"

...
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 2Gi
  requests:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 2Gi

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
